description: Draws an angle arc at a vertex between two rays, supporting interior, exterior, reflex, and right angle types with automatic 90-degree square marker detection. When two lines are parallel, returns value 0 using midpoint of line1 as vertex.
utility: Create angle arc visualization at vertex between two rays

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: Vertex point where angle is measured (or item() extraction)

arguments.name: point1
arguments.description: First ray endpoint (if not using item())

arguments.name: point2
arguments.description: Second ray endpoint (if not using item())

arguments.name: arcsize
arguments.description: Size of angle arc in pixels, e.g. arcsize(35). Default: arcsize(35)

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: Angle type: interior (default), ext1/ext2 (exterior), reflex (>180), right (force square marker)

alternates.description: Angle arc at vertex between two points
Angle at first vertex of triangle
Angle arc with explicit arc size

examples.scenario: Extract angle from a SAS triangle for sine law proof
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(-10, 11.4), size(25), range(-1, 8), grid(noaxes), type(geometry)); triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, 40, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0)); angle_A = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 1))

examples.scenario: Extract all three angles of a triangle and label them
examples.expression: angle_A = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 1)); label_A = label(graph_1, at(angle_A), "A", buff(-1.0, 1.0)); angle_B = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 2)); label_B = label(graph_1, at(angle_B), "B", buff(-1, -2.3)); angle_C = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 3)); label_C = label(graph_1, at(angle_C), "C", buff(0, -0.5))

examples.scenario: Right angle with interior type and radius for Pythagorean theorem
examples.expression: graph_2 = g2d(at(45,31), 17.7, 24.7, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 5, 1), type(uniform), grid(noaxes)); triangle_2 = sss(graph_2, 3, 4, point(graph_2, 0, 0), t(1)); angle_2 = angle(graph_2, item(triangle_2, type(angle), 1), arcsize(35), type(interior), c(black), t(0.5))

examples.scenario: Angle between two line segments with arrowhead styling
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(3,14), size(21.5), range(-1, 6), type(geometry)); line_1 = line(graph_1, point(graph_1, 0.0, 0.0), point(graph_1, 5.0, 3.0), type(segment), c(steelblue)); line_2 = line(graph_1, point(graph_1, 0.0, 0.0), point(graph_1, 5.0, 0.0), type(segment), c(steelblue)); angle_1 = angle(graph_1, line_1, line_2, type(interior), arcsize(35), arrowhead(start), c(darkred))

examples.scenario: Angle between tangent and normal lines on a plot with custom arc size
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,7), 21.5, 32.4, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
plot_1=plot(graph_1,"1/(x)",range(-5,5))
tangent_1 = tangent(graph_1, plot_1, 1)
normal_1 = normal(graph_1, plot_1, 1)
angle_1=angle(graph_1,tangent_1,normal_1,arcsize(35))

examples.scenario: Right angle marker from perpendicular projection
examples.expression: foot = project(G, side_line, P); angle_right = angle(G, foot, P, A, arcsize(35), type(right), c(blue))

examples.scenario: Animated angle using variable for SAS triangle
examples.expression: mathexpr_1 = variableanimator(at(1,4), "t", 20, 120); graph_1 = g2d(at(2,18), size(25.9), range(-6, 7), type(geometry)); triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, t, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0)); angle_A = angle(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(angle), 1)); playanim_1 = playanimator(mathexpr_1, t(3))

examples.scenario: Animated angle on unit circle with arrowhead
examples.expression:
varanim_t = variableanimator(at(2, 30), "t", 0, 360)
G = g2d(at(4, 1), size(20), range(-2, 2), type(geometry), grid())
O = point(G, 0, 0, s(3))
uc = circle(G, O, radius(1), c(steelblue), s(2))
P = anglepoint(G, uc, t, c(crimson), s(5))
xaxis_pt = point(G, 1, 0, defer())
angle_1 = angle(G, O, xaxis_pt, P, arcsize(35), arrowhead(start), c(orange), s(3))
play_anim = playanimator(varanim_t, t(8))

notes: Preferred over arc() for all angle marking. Use angle() whenever you need to show an angle at a vertex — it supports arrowhead(), arcsize(), type (interior/exterior/reflex/right), and works with animated variables.

For unit circle or rotation angles, define a reference point on the positive x-axis (e.g. point(G, 1, 0, defer())) as one ray endpoint, the origin as vertex, and the moving point as the other ray endpoint.
